segments.text: A man named Micah from the hill country of Ephraim
told his mother, {
Those eleven hundred shekels of silver that were stolen from you and that I heard you curse—I've got the silver. I was the one who took it.
segments.speaker: Micah

segments.text: Then his mother said,
My son, the Lord bless you!
segments.speaker: Mother

segments.text: He gave back to his mother the eleven hundred shekels of silver. His mother announced, {
I'm completely dedicating this money to the Lord. I'm handing it over to my son to have an idol carved, an image made with molten silver. [^56] So now I return it to you.
segments.speaker: Mother

segments.text: After he'd returned the silver to his mother she gave two hundred shekels to a silversmith who made it into a carved idol, an image made with molten silver. They were kept in Micah's house.
Micah had built a shrine for the idol. He also made an ephod and some household gods, and ordained one of his sons as his priest.
At that time Israel didn't have a king— everyone did what they themselves thought was the right thing to do. [^57]
One young man, a Levite from the tribe of Judah[^58] had been living in Bethlehem in Judah,
left Bethlehem to look for a different place to live. As he traveled through the hill country of Ephraim, he came to Micah's house.
Where are you from?
segments.speaker: Micah

segments.text: Micah asked him.
I'm a Levite from Bethlehem in Judah,
segments.speaker: Micah

segments.text: the man replied.
I'm looking for a place to live.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Come and stay here with me. You can be my ‘father’ and priest, and I'll give you ten shekels of silver a year, plus your clothes and food.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: So the Levite went inside
and agreed to stay with him. The young man became like a son to him.
Micah ordained the Levite as his own priest and he lived in Micah's house.
I'm sure the Lord will bless me now, because I have a Levite as my priest,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Micah concluded.
